notes: Full fingers are better than trimmed or damaged fingers.

rule: Power components do not equal high precious metal value
action: Recover copper, aluminum, transformers, or wire only if labor is worth it.
notes: Large components can fool visual AI into overgrading.
Processor damage affects grade but does not always eliminate value.

rule: Server and telecom logic boards should be manually reviewed
action: Separate from mixed boards and review before selling.
notes: These boards often carry better recovery value than common consumer boards.

rule: Green board alone is not enough for high grade
positive_signals: green PCB plus dense IC layout
negative_signals: green PCB with power components only
action: Use supporting signals before upgrading grade.
notes: Board color is only one clue, not proof of value.

rule: RAM should be identified by shape and fingers
action: Sort RAM separately from mixed boards.
notes: Server RAM may grade above common desktop RAM.

rule: Low density boards should not route to Pay Dirt
action: Keep Pay Dirt off unless a special component is detected.
